comment_text.sn35.68:1.2: The parallel at SA 230 has, “As for the world, why is it called the world?” (世間者，云何名世.

root_text.sn35.68:1.2: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, loko vā assa lokapaññatti vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.68:1.3: “Yattha kho, samiddhi, atthi cakkhu, atthi rūpā, atthi cakkhuviññāṇaṁ, atthi cakkhuviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, atthi tattha loko vā lokapaññatti vāti …pe…
root_text.sn35.68:1.5: atthi mano, atthi dhammā, atthi manoviññāṇaṁ, atthi manoviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, atthi tattha loko vā lokapaññatti vā.
root_text.sn35.68:2.1: Yattha ca kho, samiddhi, natthi cakkhu, natthi rūpā, natthi cakkhuviññāṇaṁ, natthi cakkhuviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, natthi tattha loko vā lokapaññatti vā …pe…
root_text.sn35.68:2.3: natthi mano, natthi dhammā, natthi manoviññāṇaṁ, natthi manoviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, natthi tattha loko vā lokapaññatti vā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.68:1.1: “Sir, they speak of this thing called ‘the world’.
translation_text.sn35.68:1.2: How do we define the world or what is known as the world?”
translation_text.sn35.68:1.3: “Samiddhi, where there is the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and things knowable by eye consciousness, there is the world or what is known as the world.
translation_text.sn35.68:1.4: Where there is the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.68:1.5: Where there is the mind, ideas, mind consciousness, and things knowable by mind consciousness, there is the world or what is known as the world.
translation_text.sn35.68:2.1: Where there is no eye, no sights, no eye consciousness, and no things knowable by eye consciousness, there is no world or what is known as the world.
translation_text.sn35.68:2.2: Where there is no ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.68:2.3: Where there is no mind, no ideas, no mind consciousness, and no things knowable by mind consciousness, there is no world or what is known as the world.”
